# Your Cold Email Strategy Is Missing 3 of These 4 Campaigns
**Channel:** Nick Abraham
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qIGiBX_REPw
**Date:** 2026-04-12
**Content source:** auto-generated transcript

## Summary
Nick Abraham breaks down the four campaign segmentation strategy for cold email launches that most B2B outreach teams are missing. Rather than putting all contacts into a single campaign, segmenting by email validity type allows you to analyze performance accurately, identify issues at scale, and optimize messaging for each contact segment. His agency manages over 1,000 active clients sending 4+ million cold emails monthly using this methodology.

## Key Points & Strategies

- **Segment by email validity type** – Don't put all contacts in one campaign; separate them into distinct campaigns based on verification results
- **Campaign 1: Valid SMTP** – Emails verified as valid typically underperform compared to other segments because they get hammered the most in cold email
- **Campaign 2: Valid Catch-Alls** – Separate validated catch-all emails into their own campaign; set bounce rate safeguards (5% threshold) to pause underperforming segments
- **Campaign 3: Public Emails** – Target info@, hello@, contact@, support@, sales@ addresses; particularly effective for SMB and local business outreach with generic openers
- **Campaign 4: Custom Enrichment/Manual Crawl** – For accounts without database matches, use web scraping tools to extract names and titles from about, team, career, and leadership pages
- **Use waterfall enrichment** – Increase email coverage before campaign launch
- **Tailor copy by segment** – Public email campaigns require different messaging (generic openers) since you lack first names
- **Monitor comparative performance** – Track reply rates and positive responses by segment to inform future outreach strategy

## Workflows & Tools Discussed

- **Account Building:** Disco (account lists)
- **Contact Databases:** Apollo, ListKit, Prospect.io
- **Email Verification:** MillionVerifier
- **Catch-All Validation:** Bounce Ban, Scrubby (formerly owned by Nick)
- **Campaign Management:** SmartLead (bounce rate safeguards)
- **Web Scraping:** Watercrawl (extract names/titles from websites)
- **AI Content Generation:** Jasper AI, ClayGen (scraping automation)
- **Email Sending Volume:** 4M+ cold emails/month, 1M+ LinkedIn DMs/month, 100K+ cold calls/month

## Use Cases Covered

- **SMB and Mid-Market Outreach** – Public email campaigns perform well for local businesses and smaller accounts
- **Enterprise SaaS** – Different strategy (public email campaign not recommended)
- **Catch-All Validation** – Industry-specific bounce rates (20%+ in some industries)
- **Performance Benchmarking** – Comparing reply rates across segments (e.g., 1% SMTP valid vs. 1.7% public email)
- **Contact Discovery** – Four-tier approach for accounts with no database matches

## Quotes Worth Noting

> "You want to segment it based off of the data that you have so that it's easier to analyze the campaign's results and really be able to group them according to, you know, if they're a catch-all or valid email and so forth so it's easier to spot issues at scale."

> "Whenever you identify that it has a high bounce rate, you can just shut it off because then you can make the assumption that for this industry and for this offer and for this group of contacts that I'm hitting, the valid catch-alls aren't as actually or aren't as valid as the tool is telling me."
